title: Drahi Is Playing an Old Game in the US With $22 Billion Debt
summary: If Patrick Drahi’s recent restructuring of his French telecoms business showed how far certain borrowers can squeeze their lenders, just look at what he is doing on the other side of the pond. The tycoon has pushed holders of $22 billion of debt in another of his companies into an extremely tight corner and once again the fine print may be on his side.

title: US Stock Futures Bounce After Market Pullback; Oracle Declines
summary: US equity futures advanced, bouncing after Wednesday’s late selloff in tech shares, while a swift end to renewed American strikes on Iran kept oil prices in check.

title: Junk Debt Divide Deepens as Pimco Joins in Warning of Stress
summary: Fears of a stagflation shock from the Middle East conflict are increasingly souring investor sentiment toward the weakest global corporate borrowers, many of which binged on cheap debt during the era of ultra-low interest rates.

title: Why Indonesia’s Rupiah Keeps Hitting Record Lows
summary: Indonesia’s rupiah — Asia’s worst-performing currency this year — has been under pressure since late 2024 when investors began reassessing the country’s fiscal outlook following the election of President Prabowo Subianto. Before this current period of volatility, the currency hadn’t traded at such weak levels since the Asian Financial Crisis of 1997-98.

title: How Houthis’ Red Sea Shipping Threat Risks Bigger Oil Shock
summary: One thing that’s helped limit the Iran war’s disruption to global oil supply is the ability of Saudi Arabia to use an alternative export route to the Strait of Hormuz: the Red Sea.

title: Blackstone to Back Two Sigma Veteran’s Quant Hedge Fund Startup
summary: Jinger Zhao, a former Two Sigma Investments managing director who spent 18 years at the asset manager, is preparing to start her own Asia-focused hedge fund with initial backing from Blackstone Inc.

title: Copper Declines as Fresh US-Iran Flareup Stokes Inflation Fears
summary: Copper inched lower for a third day as fresh fighting in the Middle East raised concern around faster inflation, higher interest rates and slower economic growth.

title: US, Turkey Resolve Key Issue on State Bank Ahead of Trump Visit
summary: The US moved to resolve a key legal dispute with Turkey involving a state bank, alleviating a major irritant in bilateral ties ahead of President Donald Trump’s anticipated visit next month.

title: Stocks Rebound, Oil Falls as US Ends Iran Strikes: Markets Wrap
summary: Traders are buying the dip in stocks as a swift conclusion to the latest round of US strikes against Iran raised expectations that talks to reopen the Strait of Hormuz will get back on track.

title: Italy’s Meloni Says EU Must Find Envoy to Speak to Russia
summary: Italian Premier Giorgia Meloni called on the European Union to appoint a representative to negotiate peace with Russia, inserting her voice into the debate over about how to engage with Vladimir Putin.

title: UK Watchdog Probes Audit Firms of Failed Mortgage Lender MFS
summary: The UK’s audit watchdog is probing accountancy firms linked to Market Financial Solutions Ltd., the mortgage lender backed by multiple Wall Street banks that collapsed in February.

title: Moody’s Cuts UK Budget Hotel Chain Travelodge Deeper Into Junk
summary: Moody’s Ratings slashed Travelodge Hotels Ltd’s credit rating deeper into junk, citing the company’s weaker financial performance and deteriorating debt metrics.

title: Alibaba, JD Lead China Selloff After Latest Beijing Warning
summary: Shares of Alibaba Group Holding Ltd. and JD.com Inc. slid after Chinese regulators scolded leading e-commerce players for what it called misleading promotions, Beijing’s latest warning against unchecked competition in the vast online arena.

title: Hanuman Wind Pulls Bond Again as Parent Governance Clouds
summary: Hanuman Wind Power, a Hong Kong-based unit of troubled Thai renewable energy firm Energy Absolute Pcl, postponed a junk bond offering for a second time after it failed to attract the desired investor demand, according to a person familiar with the matter who asked not to be identified.

title: Oil Erases Gain as Hormuz Workarounds Offset New US-Iran Strikes
summary: Oil erased an earlier increase as concerns over fresh attacks between the US and Iran were offset by continued signs of rising oil flows through the Strait of Hormuz and depressed Chinese consumption.

title: Probably a Bit More Markets Pain Ahead: 3-Minutes MLIV
summary: Anna Edwards, Guy Johnson and Mark Cudmore break down today's key themes for analysts and investors on "Bloomberg: The Opening Trade."  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Gilt Traders Brace for UK Vote With Burnham Eyeing Starmer’s Job
summary: Bond investors including BNP Paribas Asset Management, Neuberger Berman and Allspring Global Investments are predicting a vote in a small northern constituency will ignite another bout of volatility in the UK’s $3 trillion debt market.

title: Frasers Makes $2.3 Billion Offer to Buy Rest of Hugo Boss
summary: Frasers Group Plc offered to buy the rest of Hugo Boss AG for about €2 billion ($2.3 billion) as billionaire Mike Ashley seeks to add another fashion brand to his growing collection.

title: SpaceX Euphoria Tests Markets as War Risks Rise | Insight with Haslinda Amin 06/11/2026
summary: Insight with Haslinda Amin, a daily news program featuring in-depth, high-profile interviews and analysis to give viewers the complete picture on the stories that matter. The show features prominent leaders spanning the worlds of business, finance, politics and culture. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Ryanair Probed Over Charging Parents to Sit With Children
summary: Ryanair Holdings Plc. is facing a consumer investigation over fees that parents have to pay to sit next to their children.

title: Wizz Air Says Iran Conflict Cut into Earnings; Omits Outlook
summary: Wizz Air Holdings Plc said the Iran conflict shaved €50 million from earnings in its fiscal full year, almost wiping out profit in the period, while vowing to take advantage of market dislocations to push for growth.

title: China Firm Secures Kenya Airport Revamp Deal After Adani Fallout
summary: Kenya picked a Chinese firm for a $2.9 billion upgrade of its biggest airport, people with knowledge of the matter said, two years after a concession deal with Indian conglomerate Adani Group was canceled.

title: Horizons Middle East & Africa 6/11/2026
summary: Horizons Middle East & Africa is your daily spotlight on one of the world's fastest-growing regions. Live from Dubai, we bring you the latest global markets and analysis, plus news-making interviews, with a special focus on MEA. All that and more, as you head to the office in the Gulf, pause for lunch in Hong Kong, or start your day in London or Johannesburg.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Indian Banks Look to Foreign Funding to Maximize RBI’s Bazooka
summary: Some Indian lenders have asked the central bank if they can help individuals access loans from overseas banks to bring in more capital under a new plan, according to people familiar with the matter, potentially amplifying inflows into the country.

title: Tech Rout Pushes China Stocks to Brink of Bear Market | 6/11/2026
summary: “Bloomberg: The China Show” is your definitive source for news and analysis on the world's second-biggest economy. From politics and policy to tech and trends, Yvonne Man and David Ingles give global investors unique insight, delivering in-depth discussions with the newsmakers who matter.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: ECB Set to Raise Rates for First Time Since 2023
summary: The European Central Bank is set to raise interest rates for the first time since 2023, judging it can no longer ignore the upswing in inflation caused by the Iran war. Bloomberg's Oliver Crook is in Frankfurt with a preview of today's decision. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Indonesia Market Rout Resumes as Interest Rate Hike Bets Mount
summary: Indonesia’s bonds and stocks resumed their selloff Thursday, underscoring expectations that the central bank will have to raise interest-rates again to limit capital outflows.

title: Gold Whipsaws in Choppy Trading as US Completes New Iran Strikes
summary: Gold whipsawed after the US completed a fresh round of strikes against Iran, raising the stakes in a war that’s roiled global markets and stoked inflation.

title: Kuwait Ships Cooking Gas Out of Hormuz as Gulf Producers Go Dark
summary: Kuwait has shipped a cargo of liquefied petroleum gas out of the Persian Gulf through the Strait of Hormuz using a tanker it controls, as more producers opt for clandestine tactics to get energy to markets.
